content.thinking: Let me start by understanding the current state of the RabbitMQ cluster and the TLS configuration.

content.text: I'll start by inspecting the current cluster state and TLS configuration.

input.command:
NS="karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd"
kubectl -n $NS get pods
kubectl -n $NS get secrets
input.description: Get pods and secrets in the namespace

input.command:
NS="karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd"
# Inspect current TLS secret
kubectl -n $NS get secret rabbitmq-tls -o jsonpath='{.data}' | python3 -c "import sys,json,base64; d=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(k,':\n',base64.b64decode(v).decode()) for k,v in d.items()]"
input.description: Decode and inspect current TLS secret contents

content.thinking:
The current certificate expires in 1 day (June 27, 2026). I need to create a new leaf certificate that is valid for at least 300 days.

I have the CA private key in `rabbitmq-tls-ca` secret. Let me get it and use it to sign a new leaf certificate with the same SANs but a longer validity.

Let me get the CA key and CA cert, then generate a new leaf cert.

content.text: The current cert expires in 1 day. I'll generate a new leaf cert (300+ days) using the CA key from the secret, then update the secret and restart pods to pick it up.

input.command:
NS="karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd"
# Get the CA key
kubectl -n $NS get secret rabbitmq-tls-ca -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.key}' | base64 -d > /tmp/ca.key
ls -la /tmp/ca.key
input.description: Extract CA private key from secret

input.command:
NS="karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd"
# Extract CA cert
kubectl -n $NS get secret rabbitmq-tls -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' | base64 -d > /tmp/ca.crt

# Generate new leaf key and CSR
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/tls.key 2048

# Create SAN config matching the existing cert
cat > /tmp/leaf.cnf << 'EOF'
[req]
default_bits = 2048
prompt = no
distinguished_name = dn
req_extensions = req_ext

[dn]
CN = rabbitmq

[req_ext]
subjectAltName = @alt_names

[alt_names]
DNS.1 = rabbitmq
DNS.2 = rabbitmq.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd
DNS.3 = rabbitmq.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc
DNS.4 = rabbitmq.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = rabbitmq-headless
DNS.6 = rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd
DNS.7 = rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc
DNS.8 = rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc.cluster.local
DNS.9 = rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc.cluster.local
DNS.10 = rabbitmq-1.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc.cluster.local
DNS.11 = rabbitmq-2.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc.cluster.local
EOF

openssl req -new -key /tmp/tls.key -out /tmp/tls.csr -config /tmp/leaf.cnf

echo "CSR generated:"
openssl req -noout -subject -in /tmp/tls.csr
input.description: Generate new leaf key and CSR with all required SANs

input.command:
# Sign the CSR with the CA for 365 days (> 300)
cat > /tmp/v3_ext.cnf << 'EOF'
subjectAltName = @alt_names
keyUsage = critical, digitalSignature, keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth, clientAuth

[alt_names]
DNS.1 = rabbitmq
DNS.2 = rabbitmq.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd
DNS.3 = rabbitmq.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc
DNS.4 = rabbitmq.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = rabbitmq-headless
DNS.6 = rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd
DNS.7 = rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc
DNS.8 = rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc.cluster.local
DNS.9 = rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc.cluster.local
DNS.10 = rabbitmq-1.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc.cluster.local
DNS.11 = rabbitmq-2.rabbitmq-headless.karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd.svc.cluster.local
EOF

openssl x509 -req \
  -in /tmp/tls.csr \
  -CA /tmp/ca.crt \
  -CAkey /tmp/ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/tls.crt \
  -days 365 \
  -extfile /tmp/v3_ext.cnf

echo "New cert info:"
openssl x509 -noout -dates -subject -in /tmp/tls.crt
input.description: Sign the CSR with CA to produce 365-day leaf certificate

content.thinking: The new cert is valid and signed by the same CA. Now I need to update the `rabbitmq-tls` secret with the new cert and key, keeping `ca.crt` the same.

content.text: New cert verifies against CA. Now update the secret with the new leaf cert and key.

input.command:
NS="karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd"
# Update the TLS secret with new cert and key (keep ca.crt unchanged)
kubectl -n $NS create secret generic rabbitmq-tls \
  --from-file=ca.crt=/tmp/ca.crt \
  --from-file=tls.crt=/tmp/tls.crt \
  --from-file=tls.key=/tmp/tls.key \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl -n $NS apply -f -
input.description: Update rabbitmq-tls secret with new leaf cert and key

input.command:
NS="karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd"
# Check how RabbitMQ is deployed - StatefulSet needs a rolling restart
kubectl -n $NS get statefulset rabbitmq -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.volumes}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; v=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(x) for x in v]"
input.description: Check how TLS secret is mounted in RabbitMQ pods

input.command:
NS="karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd"
# Check mount path for TLS
kubectl -n $NS get statefulset rabbitmq -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].volumeMounts}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; v=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(x) for x in v]"
input.description: Check volume mount path for TLS secret

content.thinking:
The TLS secret is mounted at `/etc/rabbitmq/tls`. Kubernetes will automatically update the mounted files from the secret within a minute or so (via kubelet sync). However, RabbitMQ won't pick up the new cert files automatically - we need to restart the pods.

Let me do a rolling restart of the StatefulSet to ensure all pods pick up the new cert.

content.text: The TLS secret is mounted at `/etc/rabbitmq/tls`. I'll trigger a rolling restart so all pods reload the new cert.

input.command:
NS="karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd"
kubectl -n $NS rollout restart statefulset/rabbitmq
echo "Waiting for rollout..."
input.description: Trigger rolling restart of RabbitMQ StatefulSet

input.command:
NS="karma-rabbitmq-manual-tls-rotation-20-a3ae18dd"
# Poll until rollout is done
for i in $(seq 1 36); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n $NS get pods --no-headers 2>/dev/null)
  echo "=== $(date) ==="
  echo "$STATUS"
  # Check if all 3 pods are Running and Ready
  READY=$(echo "$STATUS" | grep -c "1/1.*Running" || true)
  if [ "$READY" -eq 3 ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods Running and Ready!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Poll until all 3 RabbitMQ pods are ready
